title: manifest.json — mutable envelope for one track
description: Index of analysis documents plus all run metadata. This is the ONLY file in a track directory permitted to differ between two runs on identical input+config; every volatile value (timestamps, durations, host, device, tool version) lives here and nowhere else.

title.description: Display title. From --title at ingest, else container tags, else the source filename stem. Advisory, not identity.

artist.description: Optional, same provenance rules as title.

created_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 time of ingest. Run metadata — permitted here only.

documents.description: One fixed-name entry per pipeline document. Fixed keys (not a dynamic map) so Swift can decode a concrete struct.

stems.description: Stem artifact status (stems are files, not a JSON document, so they are tracked here rather than in documents).

retained.description: false = stems were generated for dependent stages but deleted per config; a re-run of a dependent stage must regenerate them.

error.description: Present when status is failed: one-line cause + truncated stderr. (Added in 1.1.0.)

warning.description: Present when the stage succeeded with a caveat a batch operator should see — e.g. the MPS-failure reason behind an automatic CPU fallback. (Added in 1.2.0.)

status.description: pending = never run; stale = present but produced under a different config_hash than current config; not_applicable = e.g. video stage on an audio-only track. Absence of a document file is disambiguated by this field.

config_hash.description: Hash of the canonical JSON of THIS STAGE's config subset, so unrelated config edits don't mark a document stale.

run_metadata.description: Volatile provenance for the most recent successful run of the stage.

api_usage.description: Present when the run made external API calls (M5+): the cost ledger entry. estimated_usd is the pre-flight estimate for estimate-vs-actual comparison. (Added in 1.3.0.)

device.description: Present for neural stages (stems, lyrics). Documents are byte-identical across devices; stem FILES are guaranteed identical only on cpu with stems.cpu_threads = 1 (see PLAN §7 / OQ-13). cpu here may be the result of an automatic MPS-failure fallback — see the stems block warning field.

path.description: Path relative to the track directory. Present when status is ok or stale.

content_sha256.description: Hash of the document file as written — the determinism-audit handle.

error.description: Present when status is failed: one-line cause + truncated stderr.
